declarations.name: Documentation
declarations.default:
{ render: () => html`

VSCode Button Component

A comprehensive button component that follows VSCode's design system. Built with Lit and designed for modern web applications that need VSCode-style interfaces.

Key Features

  • Three Variants: Primary, Secondary, and Tertiary styles
  • Multiple Sizes: Small, Medium, and Large options
  • Icon Support: Before, after, or icon-only configurations
  • Animation: Spinning icons with customizable duration
  • Form Integration: Full form support with type, name, and value attributes
  • Accessibility: ARIA compliant with proper focus management
  • Fixed Width: Consistent sizing for button groups

Basic Usage

<vscode-button>Click me</vscode-button>
<vscode-button variant="secondary">Secondary</vscode-button>
<vscode-button icon="add">Add Item</vscode-button>
<vscode-button icon="settings" icon-only></vscode-button>
Click me Secondary Add Item

Design Guidelines

When to Use Each Variant

Primary Primary

Main actions, call-to-action buttons, form submissions. Use sparingly - typically one per view.

Secondary Secondary

Supporting actions, cancel buttons, alternative options. Most common button type.

Tertiary Tertiary

Subtle actions, links, less important actions. Minimal visual weight.

Icon Guidelines

  • Use icon-only buttons for toolbars and compact spaces
  • Always provide a title attribute for icon-only buttons
  • Use spinning icons for loading states and progress indicators
  • Prefer icon before text for most actions, icon after for navigation
Save File Next Step Loading...

Accessibility

  • Keyboard navigation with Tab and Enter/Space
  • Screen reader support with proper ARIA attributes
  • Focus indicators follow VSCode's focus ring style
  • Disabled state properly communicated to assistive technologies
  • Icon-only buttons require title attributes for context

Properties Reference

Explore the interactive controls below to see all available properties and their effects. Check out the All Variations story for a comprehensive showcase of different combinations.

💡 Tip: Use the Controls panel below to experiment with different property combinations and see how they affect the button's appearance and behavior.

This story provides an overview of features, design guidelines, accessibility considerations, and best practices.', }, }, controls: {disable: true}, // Disable controls for documentation story }, }

declarations.name: AllProperties
declarations.default:
{ render: () => html`

All Button Properties Showcase

This story demonstrates every available property of the VSCode Button component with visual examples and descriptions.

Core Appearance Properties

variant: 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' (default: 'primary')

Controls the visual style and hierarchy of the button.

Primary Button Secondary Button Tertiary Button

size: 'small' | 'medium' | 'large' (default: 'medium')

Controls the overall size and padding of the button.

Small Button Medium Button Large Button

disabled: boolean (default: false)

Disables the button, making it non-interactive and visually muted.

Enabled Button Disabled Button Disabled Secondary Disabled Tertiary

Icon Properties

icon: string (default: '')

A Codicon name to display before the button text. Leave empty for no icon.

No Icon Add Item Save File Settings Delete

iconAfter: string (default: '')

A Codicon name to display after the button text. Useful for navigation or directional actions.

Next Step Open External Dropdown Save & Continue

iconOnly: boolean (default: false)

Shows only the icon without text. Perfect for toolbars and compact interfaces. Always include a title attribute.

iconSpin: boolean, iconSpinDuration: number (default: false, undefined)

Makes the icon spin continuously. Duration controls animation speed in milliseconds.

Loading... Slow Spin (2s) Fast Spin (0.5s)

iconAfterSpin: boolean, iconAfterSpinDuration: number (default: false, undefined)

Makes the after icon spin continuously. Duration controls animation speed in milliseconds.

Processing Custom Duration Save & Sync

Layout Properties

fixed: boolean (default: false)

Makes the button take full width of its container. Useful for consistent button groups.

Fixed Width Buttons:

Save Cancel Apply Changes Now

Regular Width Buttons:

Save Cancel Apply Changes Now

Form Properties

type: 'button' | 'submit' | 'reset' (default: 'button')

Controls the button's behavior in forms. Submit triggers form submission, reset clears form data.

Regular Button Submit Form Reset Form

name: string (default: undefined)

The name attribute for form submission. Used to identify which button was clicked.

Save Save as Draft Delete

value: string (default: '')

The value sent with form submission when this button is clicked.

High Priority Medium Priority Low Priority

Accessibility & Internal Properties

autofocus: boolean (default: false)

Automatically focuses the button when the page loads. Only one element should have autofocus per page.

Auto-focused Button Regular Button

role: string (default: 'button', internal)

ARIA role for accessibility. Automatically set to 'button' for screen readers.

Default Role (button) (All buttons automatically have role="button")

tabIndex: number (default: 0, internal)

Controls tab order for keyboard navigation. Automatically managed (disabled buttons get tabIndex=-1).

Tab Order 1 Tab Order 2 Disabled (no tab) Tab Order 3

focused: boolean (default: false, internal)

Reflects the current focus state. Automatically managed by focus/blur event handlers.

Click to focus (Focus state is automatically managed)

declarations.default:
{ render: () => html`

VSCode Checkbox Component

📦 VSCode Elements Component

This is an unmodified component from VSCode Elements, providing authentic VSCode styling and behavior. It maintains full compatibility with the original VSCode Elements library.

A versatile checkbox component that allows users to select one or more options from a set. Supports both traditional checkbox and modern toggle switch modes with comprehensive form integration.

Key Features

  • Dual Modes: Traditional checkbox and modern toggle switch
  • Three States: Unchecked, checked, and indeterminate
  • Form Integration: Full form support with validation and required fields
  • Accessibility: ARIA compliant with keyboard navigation and screen reader support
  • Validation States: Invalid/error styling for form validation
  • Flexible Labeling: Support for both attribute and slot-based labels

Basic Usage

<vscode-checkbox>Accept terms</vscode-checkbox>
<vscode-checkbox checked>Pre-selected option</vscode-checkbox>
<vscode-checkbox toggle>Enable feature</vscode-checkbox>
<vscode-checkbox indeterminate>Partial selection</vscode-checkbox>
Accept terms and conditions Pre-selected option Enable dark mode Partial selection

Mode Comparison

Checkbox Mode (Default)

Option 1 Option 2 (selected) Option 3 (partial)

Traditional checkbox appearance. Best for multiple selections and forms.

Toggle Mode

Enable notifications Auto-save enabled Premium feature

Modern toggle switch appearance. Perfect for settings and on/off states.

States and Validation

Basic States

Normal Checked Disabled Checked & Disabled

Validation States

Required field Invalid state Indeterminate Invalid & Checked

Toggle States

Toggle off Toggle on Toggle disabled Toggle invalid

Form Integration

Form Fields with Names and Values

Enable email notifications Receive product updates Marketing communications

Required Field Validation

I accept the terms and conditions * This field is required for form submission

Use Cases and Examples

Settings Panel

Auto-save files Word wrap Show minimap Line numbers

Multi-Select List

Select All Languages
JavaScript TypeScript Python Rust

Accessibility

  • Keyboard navigation with Space to toggle and Tab to navigate
  • Screen reader support with proper ARIA attributes and roles
  • Focus indicators follow VSCode's focus ring style
  • Form validation integration with native HTML5 validation
  • Toggle mode uses proper switch role for assistive technologies
  • Indeterminate state properly communicated to screen readers

Properties Reference

Explore the interactive controls below to see all available properties and their effects. Check out the other stories for specific use cases and examples.

💡 Tip: This component is part of the VSCode Elements library and maintains full compatibility with the original implementation. For detailed API documentation, visit the VSCode Elements documentation.

members.description: The element's initial checked state, which will be restored when the containing form is reset.

members.name: toggle
members.description: When true, renders as a toggle switch instead of a checkbox.

members.name: value
members.description:
Associate a value to the checkbox. According to the native checkbox [specification](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/input/checkbox#value_2), If the component participates in a form:

- If it is unchecked, the value will not be submitted.
- If it is checked but the value is not set, `on` will be submitted.
- If it is checked and value is set, the value will be submitted.

members.name: checkValidity
members.description:
Returns `true` if the element's value is valid; otherwise, it returns `false`.
If the element's value is invalid, an invalid event is triggered on the element.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLInputElement/checkValidity)

members.name: reportValidity
members.description:
Returns `true` if the element's value is valid; otherwise, it returns `false`.
If the element's value is invalid, an invalid event is triggered on the element, and the
browser displays an error message to the user.

[MDN Reference](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/HTMLInputElement/reportValidity)

font stylesheet on the page and returns the stylesheet URL and the nonce
id.

declarations.default:
{ render: () => html`

VSCode Multi Select Component

📦 VSCode Elements Component

This is an unmodified component from VSCode Elements, providing authentic VSCode styling and behavior. It maintains full compatibility with the original VSCode Elements library.

A versatile multi-selection dropdown component that allows users to choose multiple options from a list. Features checkboxes for each option and supports both traditional select mode and combobox mode with filtering.

Key Features

  • Multiple Selection: Select multiple options with checkboxes
  • Selection Counter: Shows count of selected items in the face
  • Bulk Actions: Select All and Deselect All buttons
  • Two Modes: Traditional select and combobox with text input
  • Filtering: Type to filter options in combobox mode
  • Custom Options: Create new options on-the-fly when creatable is enabled
  • Form Integration: Full form support with validation and required fields
  • Icon Support: Display icons alongside option text
  • Accessibility: ARIA compliant with keyboard navigation

Basic Usage

<vscode-multi-select label="Choose options">
  <vscode-option value="1">Option 1</vscode-option>
  <vscode-option value="2">Option 2</vscode-option>
  <vscode-option value="3">Option 3</vscode-option>
</vscode-multi-select>
Option 1 Option 2 Option 3 JavaScript TypeScript Python
`, parameters: { docs: { description: { story: 'Complete documentation and usage guidelines for the VSCode Multi Select component. This component is an unmodified component from VSCode Elements, providing authentic VSCode styling and behavior.', }, }, controls: {disable: true}, // Disable controls for documentation story }, }

- fuzzy - The list item contains the letters of the search pattern in the same order, but at any position.

- startsWithPerTerm - The search pattern matches the beginning of any word in the searched text.

declarations.name: Documentation
declarations.default:
{ render: () => html`

VSCode Tooltip Component

A flexible tooltip component that provides contextual information on hover or focus. Designed to integrate seamlessly with VSCode's design system and accessibility standards.

Key Features

  • Rich Content: Support for custom components via slots or simple text via property
  • Multiple Positions: Top, bottom, left, and right positioning
  • Customizable Timing: Configurable show and hide delays
  • Accessibility: Full keyboard support and ARIA compliance
  • Responsive: Mobile-friendly with automatic repositioning
  • Themeable: CSS custom properties for easy customization
  • Universal: Works with any HTML element as trigger

Basic Usage

Simple text tooltip using the label property: 

 <vscode-tooltip label="Helpful information"> <vscode-button>Hover me</vscode-button> </vscode-tooltip>

Custom content using the tooltip slot: 

 <vscode-tooltip> <vscode-button>Hover me</vscode-button> <div slot="tooltip"> <vscode-icon name="info"></vscode-icon> <span>Custom component content</span> </div> </vscode-tooltip>

Examples

members.name: expandMode
members.description:
Controls how tree folders are expanded when clicked. This property is designed to use
the `workbench.tree.expandMode` setting.

Valid options are available as constants.

```javascript
import {ExpandMode} from '@vscode-elements/elements/dist/vscode-tree/vscode-tree.js';

document.querySelector('vscode-tree').expandMode = ExpandMode.singleClick;
```

members.name: hideArrows
members.description:
Although arrows are always visible in the Tree component by default in VSCode, some icon sets
(e.g., Material Icon Theme) allow disabling them in the file explorer view. This flag makes it
possible to mimic that behavior.

members.name: indent
members.description:
Controls the indentation in pixels. This property is designed to use the
`workbench.tree.indent` setting.

members.name: indentGuides
members.description:
Controls whether the tree should render indent guides. This property is
designed to use the `workbench.tree.renderIndentGuides` setting.

Valid options are available as constants.

```javascript
import {IndentGuides} from '@vscode-elements/elements/dist/vscode-tree/vscode-tree.js';

document.querySelector('vscode-tree').expandMode = IndentGuides.onHover;
```

members.name: combobox
members.description: Options can be filtered by typing into a text input field.

members.name: label
members.description:
Accessible label for screen readers. When a `<vscode-label>` is connected
to the component, it will be filled automatically.

members.name: disabled
members.description: The element cannot be used and is not focusable.

members.name: invalid
members.description: Sets the invalid state manually.

members.name: filter
members.description:
Search method in the filtered list within the combobox mode.

- contains - The list item includes the searched pattern at any position.
- fuzzy - The list item contains the letters of the search pattern in the same order, but at any position.
- startsWith - The search pattern matches the beginning of the searched text.
- startsWithPerTerm - The search pattern matches the beginning of any word in the searched text.

members.name: focused
members.description: Its value is true when element is focused.

members.name: open
members.description: Toggle the dropdown visibility.

members.name: position
members.description: Position of the options list when visible.
